characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: The leader of the group. A vain athlete whose arrogant attitude and short-sighted judgment always gets him in trouble (though he always seems to make things right at the end), Otto is usually seen to be the most driven and intense in skateboarding, surfing, etc. The Ace: He's pretty much a master of all extreme sports. In "Race Across New Zealand", he won or would have won all the trials if another contestant hadn't cheated. Except the last one, in which he gave up the win for his best friend. Aesop Amnesia: Sure he'll make things he screwed up right at the end, but he'll only end up doing something much worse the next episode. Affectionate Nickname: His friends, his father and Tito sometimes refer to him as "Rocket Boy". Always Someone Better: What Twister was to him in the episode "Total Luger". It's one of the few sports where Twister outshines Otto at. Otto actually resorts to cheating to beat him.Also, Sam in "Sam: King of Kickball". After thinking he would become team captain, he gets shocked and annoyed by Sam proving better and eventually becoming team captain instead. He turns his back on Sam after Sam doesn't pick him on the team.

characters.name: Otto Rocket
characters.description: The leader of the group. A vain athlete whose arrogant attitude and short-sighted judgment always gets him in trouble (though he always seems to make things right at the end), Otto is usually seen to be the most driven and intense in skateboarding, surfing, etc. The Ace: He's pretty much a master of all extreme sports. In "Race Across New Zealand", he won or would have won all the trials if another contestant hadn't cheated. Except the last one, in which he gave up the win for his best friend. Aesop Amnesia: Sure he'll make things he screwed up right at the end, but he'll only end up doing something much worse the next episode. Affectionate Nickname: His friends, his father and Tito sometimes refer to him as "Rocket Boy". Always Someone Better: What Twister was to him in the episode "Total Luger". It's one of the few sports where Twister outshines Otto at. Otto actually resorts to cheating to beat him.Also, Sam in "Sam: King of Kickball". After thinking he would become team captain, he gets shocked and annoyed by Sam proving better and eventually becoming team captain instead. He turns his back on Sam after Sam doesn't pick him on the team.

characters.name: Regina "Reggie" Rocket
characters.description: Otto's older sister by two years. While no less competitive and skilled as an athlete, Reggie is also of calmer disposition and great maturity. She is also an aspiring publisher and most noted for her "Zine". Action Girl: Reggie is really talented at extreme sports. Affectionate Nickname: Like her brother, she is sometimes referred to as "Rocket Girl" by Sam, Twister, Tito and Raymundo. Alliterative Name: Both her first and last name start with R. Berserk Button:Reggie does not like being called Regina, not even by Raymundo.She goes ballistic when Trent suggests that she should "definitely come watch" him and the guys play rugby.Also, any sexist comment (such as saying someone else "plays like girls") towards her is sure to get her steamed.

characters.name: Maurice "Twister" Rodriguez
characters.description: Otto's best friend who is Latino-American. An aspiring videographer, Twister is known for his lack of intelligence and is often teased by his big brother Lars. Despite his low intellect, however, he can get pretty clever at times. Asleep in Class: In "Snow Day" he attends an assembly he considers the best one he "ever stayed awake in". Attention Deficit... Ooh, Shiny!: He would sometimes let his mind meander in many occasions. Berserk Button:Don't call him Squid.Don't refer to him by his given name of Maurice. Don't call him Squid.

characters.name: Sam "The Squid" Dullard
characters.description: The fourth member of the main crew. While not as athletically gifted as the other three, he has found a niche as the brains and conscience of the group and is also a "rock-solid" goalie when they play hockey. Abusive Parents: Of the emotional kind. Sam's mother is a solidEducation MamaandMy Beloved Smotherwho puts plenty of academic pressure on him while his father is a self-centered workaholic who ignores Sam when he visits and tries to pay for Sam's love instead of showing him any affection or attention. Both of which contribute to his extreme insecurity. Accidental Athlete: Sam Dullard's goalie skills, which are discovered when hecasually swats away a hockey puck coming at him from behind. Acrofatic: Despite not being very athletic. Affectionate Nickname: The Squid.

characters.name: Ray "Raymundo" Rocket
characters.description: Otto and Reggie's single father, Ray is a widower and a surfer of some renown. He owns a restaurant named The Shore Shack, where he works alongside his friend Tito, as well as an equipment rental and repair shop called Rocket Boards. Absurd Phobia: He is terrified of dogs, especially poodles, after seeing a movie called "Attack of the 50 Foot Poodle". Amazingly Embarrassing Parents: This is largely averted, as he shares many of Otto and Reggie's interests, and they enjoy hanging out with him. Come Christmas, though, he insists on traditions that they've long since outgrown and now consider embarrassing. Anger Born of Worry: His attitude towards his kids after either of them would get into trouble that would almost cost them their lives, which is usually followed by himpunishing them. Bumbling Dad: Sometimes. He can be pretty clumsy at times.

characters.name: Tito Makani
characters.description: Ray's best friend, a Hawaiian surfer and self-styled philosopher who helps Ray run his business. Acrofatic: Combined with his age, he still is able to perform sports such as surfing. He also helps Sam, who's similarly heavyset, with learning to surf, noticing that he's struggling because he's using a board that's too small for his weight. As Long as It Sounds Foreign: Aside from the fact that "Tito" isn't a Hawaiian name, there is no letter T in the Hawaiian alphabet, although K is sometimes pronounced as a T, like in "kiu". Big Fun: His chubbiness doesn't stop him from having a good time. Catchphrase: "Aloha to that!" whenever he agrees with something another character has said.

characters.name: Lars Rodriguez
characters.description: Twister's older brother who routinely picks on Twister and the rest of the kids. Aloof Big Brother: As if bullying Twister wasn't enough, the brothers even compete against each other in hockey matches, much to their parents' dismay since it's hard to support their sons when one of them wins while the other loses. Always Someone Better: When his team plays against the main four in hockey, they often lose. Big Brother Bully: To Twister. He has a habit of "whomping" Twister every chance he gets. Big Brother Instinct: Towards Twister despite the above trope. He doesn't want his kid brother to get actually hurt and is willing to do lots of things to ensure that doesn't happen.

characters.name: Violet Stimpleton
characters.description: A friendly neighbor of the Rockets who's good friends with Ray. Cool Old Lady: Reggie looks up to her like an aunt. And the other kids see her as one too. Happily Married: With Mervin. Heroic BSoD: When her award-winning violets are accidentally destroyed by the kids. They pull her out of it by winning her a go-karting trophy. Intergenerational Friendship: With Reggie.

characters.name: Mervin Stimpleton
characters.description: Violet's grumpy, short-tempered husband. Cloud Cuckoolander: Occasionally shows weird, over-the-top behavior (obsessively hunting gophers, staking out a possibly non-existent "prowler"). No surprise that Twister believes he's a space alien in one episode. The Engineer: He's a retired electrical engineer and enjoys building models and tinkering with electronic devices. He shares this interest with Sam and subsequently gets along better with him than Otto, Reggie or Twister. Hair-Trigger Temper: It doesn't take much to set him off. Happily Married: As abrasive as he is, it's very clear that he loves his wife.

characters.name: Conroy Blanc
characters.description: The owner of Madtown Skatepark and good friends with the gang. Berserk Button: When Twister face-planted onto his new vert ramp, he isextremely angry. Cool Teacher: Becomes Otto and Twister's teacher in "Mr. B Is In The House" after getting his teaching certification over the summer. First-Name Basis: He lets the kids call him by his first name, showing how close he is to them. He puts his foot down once he becomes a teacher, making his students call him "Mr. B." while school is in session. He still lets everyone call him "Conroy" at the Skatepark, though. Hidden Depths: He comes across as a pretty chill guy whose only interest is running the skatepark, but he spent his summer earning his teaching certification.

characters.name: Eddie Prince of the Netherworld
characters.description: A friend of the gang who calls himself "Prince of the Netherworld" and wears a demonic goalie mask. Butt-Monkey: He is often mocked, bullied, and taken advantage of by other characters Creepy Good: He freaks out a lot of people with hisGothwardrobe and interests, he's also consistently a good friend to the gang despite being lower on their totem pole and occasionally taken advantage of. Dark Is Not Evil: He's consistently one of the nicest characters on the show Early-Installment Weirdness: In “Loss of Squid,” he has a red cape and a turquoise mask instead of his typical black cape and gray mask.

characters.name: Sherry
characters.description: Straight Man: To Reggie in her more hot-headed moments. Those Two Guys: Rarely seen without her friend Trish.

characters.name: Trish
characters.description: The Ace: Is said to be one of the best surfers in town; evenOttowas reluctant to face her in a surfing contest.Otto:I gotta surf againstTrish!?She's really good! Those Two Guys: Rarely seen without her friend Sherry.

characters.name: Oliver van Rossum
characters.description: Depending on the Writer: He is either Sam's other friend, or rival. Grade Skipper: He is in high school and even helps his biology teacher. However, in later appearances he is attending the same school as the main characters. Gretzky Has the Ball: When invited to play street hockey with the gang, he asks what position he'll play; the quarterback, jockey, or outfielder. He's assigned scorekeeper. Sitcom Arch-Nemesis: To Sam. But really only pertaining to contests like sandcastle building or robot battling. Other than that, Oliver gets along well with the gang and even tries out skating once in a while. Smart People Build Robots: In the episode "Extreme Nerd", he is stated to win the same robot engineering contest every year.

characters.name: Officer Shirley
characters.description: Acquainted with Emergency Services: She's pretty much on a first name basis with Ray, Tito, and the kids, and is usually the one to respond to their antics if the police have to get involved in some way. Ship Tease: She and Ray have some of this going on,although the finale has Ray marrying a relative of Tito's.

characters.name: MacKenzieBenders
characters.description: Bratty Half-Pint: Is very violent and tempermental towards everyone she meets, even towards her own parents at times. Cute, but Cacophonic: She is only 5 years old, but she tends to scream very loudly. A Day in the Limelight: The episode "Lost and Find" focuses onMacKenziebeing separated from her parents and the main four have to help her reunite with her parents. Demoted to Extra: Seldom appears after the first season with the exception of speaking roles in season two's "Double-O Twistervision" and season three’s “Card Sharked” and a cameo in Sam’s video game in “Sim Sammy.” Her parents even make a cameo without her in “Hurricane Maurice.” Enfant Terrible: She is feared among the residents of Ocean Shore for her hot temper and violent tendencies. Even her own parents are terrified of her.

characters.name: Lieutenant Tice Ryan
characters.description: Adam Westing: Dye himself was a Marine, so Ryan is essentially that type of personality as a lifeguard. Drill Sergeant Nasty: Frequently loud and barking orders, especially when training new lifeguard recruits. TheChristmas Episodeshows he's even like this when training Santa Helpers. Reasonable Authority Figure: Despite his gruff demeanor and strict enforcement of the rules, he is dedicated to the safety of everyone who sets foot on his beach. He can be quite understanding of the kids and his recruits when the situation calls for it. Secret Test of Character: A common element of his lifeguard training program is to have Tito (a former graduate) fake an emergency in the water as a final test for nearby recruits. So Proud of You: He congratulates Twister and Sam for passing theSecret Test of Characterand being the most improved of their class.

characters.name: Josh Grody
characters.description: Bitch in Sheep's Clothing: Despite Sam's efforts to be his friend, Josh is a popularity-seeker, and it's eventually revealed that he sees Sam as a "geek" and used him just to get closer to the Rocket gang. The Bully: To Sam, whom he bullied for being a nerd, who in his opinion wasn't deserve of being in such a cool group. The Friend Nobody Likes: When he joined the Rocket gang. Sam wants them to give him a chance due to the lonely experience of being a new kid, but the kid annoys them due to: his bad attempts at slang, sucking up to Otto, and picking at Sam "for simple fun." Once the team learn of Josh's plan of replacing Sam at a game, it becomes clear how little he thinks of Sam despite him being the reason for his inclusion into the group, causing the other three to lose their patience of him and remove Josh from the group altogether. I Just Want to Have Friends: Unfortunately, he only seems to want to be friends with "cool" people. He at first tries to join the gang by hanging out with Sam, but Otto, Reggie, and Twister find him obnoxious and only tolerate him because of him being "Sam's friend". After Josh reveals he doesn't even like Sam, they don't hesitate to kick him out of the group. He later tries to join up with Lars and his crew after being rejected by the gang, and presumably is met with rejection again. Jerk with a Heart of Jerk: At first, it seems that he just wanted to have friends and fit in, but even after Sam reached out to him and got the others to hang out with him, he repays him by picking on him and trying to steal his spot in the group. He's such a jerk that Sam and the others finally lose their patience with him and unfriend him.

characters.name: Clio
characters.description: Twister's fashionista cousin and a rival of Reggie. The Ace: Clio is a highly skilled figure skater and has demonstrated the ability to become anInstant Expertin sports without really training for it. She quickly masters ice hockey (though being a figure skater probably helped) and she became a surfer on par with Otto and Reggie after trying it for only one summer. Clashing Cousins: Subverted. It seems this way at first between Clio and Twister, especially after she outs his first name (Maurice) to the gang and he mentions that she bugs him a lot. Despite this, the two are very close and she visits him and her family often, and Twister eventually gets sick of Otto putting her down and defends her to his best friend. Defeat Means Friendship: Played with. Reggie and Clio challenge each other to do their respective sports with both believing they are unable to do it, but Reggie and Clio actually bothwinwhen they compete, proving each other wrong. However, this leads to them gaining a respect for each other that leads to them becoming close friends by the time of their next meeting. Embarrassing First Name: Not her, but she's the one who outs to the others that Twister's real name is Maurice,which infuriates him.
